summary: Closed, unmerged WIP PR that appears to have been opened/closed accidentally: the only human comment says, "OOPS i meant to merge yalls into mine lol". The diff was nevertheless substantial: it replaced many stub markers in src/melee/ft/ft_0C88.c with partial/common-fighter implementations around cloaking-device state, DownSpot, Barrel item carry, walk, and turn handling, and it added the supporting motion-variable union members, inline helpers, function prototypes, and more specific types needed to compile those attempts. Because the PR was not reviewed or merged and includes comments such as "rough, look at later" and large commented-out functions, treat it as evidence of exploratory matching work rather than accepted project practice.
what_changed: Expanded src/melee/ft/ft_0C88.c from mostly placeholder stub comments into a large WIP implementation block, adding bodies for functions from ftCo_800C884C through at least fn_800C9CEC while leaving later functions still as stubs.

Implemented or sketched common-fighter cloaking-device state handling in ftCo_800C88A0, ftCo_800C88D4, ftCo_800C89A0, ftCo_800C8A64, ftCo_800C8AF0, ftCo_800C8B2C, ftCo_800C8B60, and inline ftOnCloakingDevice.

Added DownSpot motion support: ftCo_800C8B74 chooses ftCo_MS_DownSpotU or ftCo_MS_DownSpotD via a new ftPickDownSpot inline, and DownSpot Anim/IASA/Phys/Coll functions call existing animation/physics/collision helpers.

Added stock/HP-related defeat or grab handling around ftCo_800C8C84, ftCo_800C8D00, fn_800C8E40, and fn_800C8E74, including special cases for Master Hand and Crazy Hand and a new Crazy Hand prototype ftCh_GrabUnk1_8015ADD0.

Added Barrel motion support in ftCo_800C9078 and ftCo_Barrel_* callbacks, storing item-derived counters from it_802960B8 and it_802960CC in motion vars and calling it_80295F38 when timers or input conditions trigger release/end behavior.
Added walk input, animation, IASA, physics, and collision code using ftWalkCommon helpers and chaining many ftCo_Attack*/catch/special/jump/dash checks before continuing walk updates.

Added turn input and turn motion support, including ftCo_Turn_CheckInput, ftCo_800C9840, fn_800C98AC, ftCo_Turn_Anim, ftCo_Turn_IASA, ftCo_Turn_Phys, ftCo_Turn_Coll, fn_800C9C2C, fn_800C9C74, and fn_800C9CEC.
Extended union ftCommon_MotionVars with new co.turn, co.barrel, and co.downspot structs, giving named access to fields at fp+2340 and nearby offsets.
Added inline helpers ftGetVictim, ftPickDownSpot, ftCheckWalkThreshold, and ftOnCloakingDevice to src/melee/ft/inlines.h.

Tightened several prototypes away from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS, including ft_8008A244 returning bool, ft_800C0098 taking Fighter*, ftColl_800787B4 taking Item_GObj* and Fighter_GObj*, ft_800C8170 taking ftCo_Fighter*, ft_800C85B8 taking ftCo_GObj*, it_80295F38 taking Item_GObj*, and Player_GetRemainingHPByIndex returning s32.

Refined ftCommonData fields from unknown types to specific types/names such as x34_walkThresholdMaybe, float x38, float x474, and GXColor x7D8_gxcolor; extended ftData_80085FD4_ret to include offsets xC, x10, and x14.
Added includes in ft_0C88.c for many common fighter action headers, ftwalkcommon, ftmetal, ftcoll, ftcolanim, ftlib, item barrel cannon, game-mode, player, lbbgflash, and lbrefract dependencies.
smart_moves: The work grouped newly discovered per-action scratch fields into ftCommon_MotionVars as action-specific structs, which made later code refer to fp->mv.co.turn, fp->mv.co.barrel, and fp->mv.co.downspot instead of raw offsets.
Several header updates replaced generic UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations with concrete argument and return types, improving callsite type pressure for matching ft_0C88.c.

The WIP used small inlines for repeated semantic checks and effects, such as ftCheckWalkThreshold and ftOnCloakingDevice, which can help reproduce compiler shape if the original code relied on inlineable helpers.

The diff preserved matching notes directly in the code, including unused stack variables, #pragma dont_inline on for ftCo_800C8D00, and a comment about preserving f2 in fn_800C9C74 through pointer aliasing.

The Barrel implementation correctly tied fighter motion vars to item helper APIs it_802960B8, it_802960CC, it_80295F38, it_8026C334, and collision linking via ftColl_800787B4, showing how common fighter state crosses into item code.
decomp_lessons: For common fighter actions, first model action-specific motion-variable layouts in ftCommon_MotionVars; this can unlock readable implementations for walk, turn, barrel, downspot, and similar common states.

Prototype refinement is often a prerequisite for matching: this WIP needed concrete Item_GObj*, Fighter_GObj*, ftCo_Fighter*, bool, s32, float*, and GXColor types across several headers before ft_0C88.c code could be expressed cleanly.

When a function only matches with unused locals or stack artifacts, record the reason narrowly. This diff contains explicit "void* unused; // needed to match" comments and a #pragma dont_inline block around a difficult function.

Do not treat every WIP semantic name as accepted terminology. Names like x34_walkThresholdMaybe, x474 // magnitude relating to walking?, and comments such as "currently unsure" are hypotheses, not project-approved facts.

Large common-action files depend on many neighboring action check headers; building includes around actual callsites can reveal missing prototypes in unrelated fighter, item, player, and game-mode headers.
Closed accidental PRs can still be useful as search indexes for symbols and matching attempts, but should not be used as evidence that a decomp approach was reviewed, accepted, or final.
naming_conventions: Used ftCo_ prefix for common fighter functions and action callbacks, e.g. ftCo_Walk_CheckInput, ftCo_Turn_Anim, ftCo_Barrel_IASA, and ftCo_DownSpot_Coll.
Used fn_800Cxxxx names for local/internal or not-yet-semantically-named functions, e.g. fn_800C8B1C, fn_800C8E40, fn_800C9058, fn_800C9290, fn_800C9C2C.
Kept address comments in prototypes, such as /* 0C884C */, /* 0C9078 */, /* 0787B4 */, and /* 295F38 */.
For motion vars, used named action substructs under fp->mv.co: turn, barrel, and downspot.
Used mixed semantic and unknown field names where knowledge was partial: direction_to, frames_to_change_direction_on_standing_turn, datAttr_x2C, datAttr_x28, item_gobj, x0_int, x18_int, and x1C_int.
Refined common-data fields with cautious names/comments rather than overclaiming, e.g. x34_walkThresholdMaybe and x474 with comment "magnitude relating to walking?".
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inserted unused locals such as void* unused and void* unused2 in several functions with comments that they were needed for matching stack/code shape.
Used #pragma push, #pragma dont_inline on, and #pragma pop around ftCo_800C8D00, indicating inlining control was necessary for a difficult match.
Left rough or not-yet-matching functions commented out, including ftCo_800C92E4 and fn_800C9528, rather than presenting them as complete.
Noted an aliasing tactic in fn_800C9C74: preserving f2 by assigning through two pointers because the compiler could not tell they referenced the same object.
Introduced local static inline threshold helpers ftCheckWalkThreshold2 and ftCheckWalkThreshold3 near turn code, likely to shape repeated comparisons while keeping readable code.
Used exact float literals and comparisons, such as 0.0F, 1.0F, 0.0 == fp->dmg.kb_applied, and direction multiplications against p_ftCommonData thresholds, to express compiler-sensitive control flow.
review_feedback: No formal review comments were present.
The only human text says: "OOPS i meant to merge yalls into mine lol", indicating the PR was likely an accidental or misdirected WIP rather than a review-ready submission.
The PR was closed within about a minute and was not merged.
Because no reviewer feedback exists, future lessons should be drawn from the diff mechanics only, not from any inferred reviewer approval.
follow_up_queries: Find the later merged PR that completed or replaced ft_0C88.c common fighter walk/turn/downspot/barrel work.
Search for accepted definitions of ftCommon_MotionVars.turn, ftCommon_MotionVars.barrel, and ftCommon_MotionVars.downspot after PR #1997.
Check whether ft_8008A244 was ultimately typed as bool ft_8008A244(Fighter_GObj*) in master.
Search for final accepted names for p_ftCommonData->x34, x38, x3C, x474, and x7D8.
Search for later review discussion on matching ftCo_800C8D00, fn_800C9528, ftCo_800C92E4, and fn_800C9C74.
Check whether ftCh_GrabUnk1_8015ADD0 was later renamed or given a semantic Crazy Hand grab/death handler name.
